title: CLI add/copy: add a --from option
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind feature

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Add a --from option to `buildah add` and `buildah copy`, mirroring the option for the Dockerfile instruction.

#### How to verify it

New integration tests!

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes #3127.

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
`buildah add` and `buildah copy` now accept a `--from` option, which can be used to copy content from other working containers or images.
```

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [nalind] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Added a few comments to the new integration tests, put five more minutes on the clock for unit tests to complete.

> LGTM
> but I'm surprised the tests added 5 minutes....
> @vrothberg @rhatdan PTAL

Along the way we had a few unit test and integration test jobs time out, so I bumped the timeouts for them to try to not have to restart them.  Red Hat's outage yesterday must have contributed to that, otherwise I wouldn't have expected the unit test run time to change.
